preamble: The realization of a glottal stop /ʔ/ as a nasal consonant /n/, typically occurring in the context of a following syllabic nasal or through the nasalization of the glottal closure.

examples.note: T-glottalization + glottal-nasal release: /t/ → [ʔ] before syllabic [n̩], and the [ʔ] is released directly into the nasal. The process is t→ʔ followed by ʔ onset into n, rather than ʔ→n per se.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2015). A Course in Phonetics (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.
